title: Stenocalyx ruber
intro: Stenocalyx ruber, also known as the "red-stemmed Stenocalyx," is a plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related diseases. Bioactive constituents of Stenocalyx ruber include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stenocalyx ruber include extracts, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Stenocalyx ruber is classified under the family Burseraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the torchwoods. This family is part of the order Sapindales, which includes various other plant families. The order Sapindales is a large and diverse group of plants that also includes the families Anacardiaceae, Rutaceae, and Meliaceae, among others. Within the family Burseraceae, Stenocalyx ruber is one of the many species that are found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Stenocalyx ruber is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis.
